title: Self-service redirect — the Tier 0 off-ramp
problem: Some visitors have a real problem but no commercial budget — civic coordination, household logistics, a hobby project. A consultant facing one of these usually either takes the job and loses money or rejects it and burns goodwill. The self-service redirect is a third move: a free, curated library of small patterns that says, in effect, 'your problem is small enough that hiring me would cost more than it is worth — here is how people solve this one themselves, come back if it outgrows you.' The visitor leaves with something usable, the goodwill is intact, and the page quietly earns long-tail search traffic.
analysis: Tier 0 replaces the binary accept/reject triage outcome with a curated off-ramp. The page lists a set of small, recognisable everyday problems, each presented as three plain blocks — the problem, a recipe to solve it, and a link to a free no-code tool that fits. The patterns are hand-picked, not generated: the value is the match, not the volume. The page opens with the principle behind it (a fifty-dollar problem deserves a free solution) and closes with an invitation to come back through the intake form when a pattern outgrows the visitor. Educating a visitor out of needing the consultant is the correct move when hiring would cost more than the problem is worth.

decisions.decision: The redirect library is curated, not algorithmic
decisions.rationale: The value of an off-ramp is the well-matched pattern, not the number of patterns. A short list of patterns the consultant actually chose feels respectful; a long auto-generated list feels dismissive.
decisions.recommendation: Add a pattern only when a real recurring request justifies it; keep each entry concrete enough to act on.

decisions.decision: Each pattern points to a free, no-code tool
decisions.rationale: A Tier 0 visitor has no budget and often no technical skill. Pointing at a spreadsheet template or a free poll tool gives them a result today without involving the consultant at all.
decisions.recommendation: Prefer well-known free tools; accept that an external tool can change its URL and keep the recipe useful even if a link rots.

implementation: src/pages/Tier0.tsx renders the page at /tier-0 (and /en/tier-0). The patterns live in src/lib/patterns.ts as a typed, bilingual array — each entry has a one-word category tag, an accent tone, a title, a problem statement, a recipe, and an optional external template link. About a dozen curated patterns ship today, covering cases such as a snow-shovelling rotation, an event RSVP poll, a small-team hours tracker, a neighbourhood lending board, shared expenses, and a few more. Each renders as a card with the three labelled blocks. The page carries an opening principle line and a closing call to action that links back to /intake. The pricing table's Tier 0 card links here directly.
successCriteria:
- A budget-mismatched visitor lands on a curated off-ramp instead of a flat rejection
- Each pattern gives the visitor a concrete recipe and a free tool to use today
- Adding or removing a pattern is a single edit to one data file
- The page invites the visitor back to /intake when a pattern outgrows them
- Reusable as a pattern for any practice that wants a Tier 0 without losing money
userGuide: Visit /tier-0 (or /en/tier-0) to browse the free pattern library. To add a pattern, append an entry to the array in src/lib/patterns.ts with a tag, tone, and bilingual title, problem, and recipe, plus an optional external template link; the page renders it automatically. To remove one, delete its entry. The Tier 0 card in the homepage pricing table links straight here.
knownLimitations: There is no click-through analytics, so the page cannot yet surface which patterns are most in demand
Patterns are defined in code — fine for a curated list of this size, but not editable without a deploy
Template links point to third-party tools, which can change their URLs over time
There is no automatic inbound flow from triage into Tier 0 — a budget mismatch does not yet route a visitor here on its own

statusHistory.reason: /tier-0 live with a curated pattern library; the pricing Tier 0 card links here.
Code-first verification — feature.json co-located at src/pages/Tier0.feature.json. The pattern library has grown to roughly a dozen entries; re-checked against patterns.ts.
